# shouldBeOpaque | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ Core Graphics ](/documentation/coregraphics)

- [ CGWindowImageOption ](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption)

- [ shouldBeOpaque ](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/shouldbeopaque)

- [ CGWindowImageOption ](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption)

-  shouldBeOpaque 

Type Property# shouldBeOpaque

Mac CatalystmacOS

```
static var shouldBeOpaque: CGWindowImageOption { get }
```

## [Discussion](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/shouldbeopaque#Discussion)

When capturing the window, partially transparent areas are backed by a solid white color so that the resulting image is fully opaque. You can combine this option with other options.

## [See Also](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/shouldbeopaque#see-also)

### [Type Properties](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/shouldbeopaque#Type-Properties)

[`static var bestResolution: CGWindowImageOption`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/bestresolution)When capturing the window, return the best image resolution. The returned image size may be different than the screen size.[`static var boundsIgnoreFraming: CGWindowImageOption`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/boundsignoreframing)[`static var nominalResolution: CGWindowImageOption`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/nominalresolution)When capturing the window, return the nominal image resolution. The returned image size is the same as the screen size.[`static var onlyShadows: CGWindowImageOption`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgwindowimageoption/onlyshadows)
